name: Oats
summary: A whole grain whose soluble fiber, beta-glucan, has some of the strongest evidence of any food for lowering LDL cholesterol.
superfoodReason: The beta-glucan in oats has a large, consistent evidence base — and an authorised health claim — for lowering LDL cholesterol.

benefits.mechanism: Oats are rich in beta-glucan, a soluble fiber that forms a thick gel in the gut. The gel traps bile acids (which are made from cholesterol) and carries them out, so the liver pulls cholesterol from the blood to make more — lowering LDL. A meta-analysis in adults with high cholesterol confirmed the lipid-lowering effect.

citations.title: Effects of oat beta-glucan intake on lipid profiles in hypercholesterolemic adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Nutrients 2022)

benefits.mechanism: The same viscous beta-glucan slows how quickly the stomach empties and carbohydrates are absorbed, blunting the post-meal glucose rise. Systematic reviews find dietary fibre and whole grains improve glycemic control in diabetes management.

citations.title: Dietary fibre and whole grains in diabetes management: systematic review and meta-analyses (Reynolds et al., PLoS Med 2020)

surprisingFacts.text: Steel-cut, rolled, and instant oats are the same grain at different processing levels — the more they're cut and flattened, the faster they digest and the higher the blood-sugar spike.

cautions.effect: A sudden jump in fibre brings bloating, gas and looser stools until your gut adjusts — increase the amount gradually and drink enough water.
